title: Gene Calls with Prodigal
author: James Thornton
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.f47bqzn
materials: Make a directory named prodigal: $ mkdir prodigal

steps.title: Login to the HPC and move into Cluster(ICE)
steps.instruction: Login to the HPC and move into Cluster(ICE). Option 3 if you have menu enabled. $ ssh hpc$ ice $ ssh hpc $ ice

steps.title: Move into your class directory
steps.instruction: Move into your class directory. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username Use YOUR username to be in the right directory

steps.title: Copy the following into a new script called run-interactive
steps.instruction: Copy the following into a new script called run-interactive.sh : #!/bin/bash#PBS -W group_list=bh_class#PBS -q windfall#PBS -l jobtype=cluster_only#PBS -l select=1:ncpus=2:mem=4gb#PBS -l walltime=24:00:00#PBS -l cput=24:00:00#PBS -l place=pack:shared#PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu#PBS -m bea Replace netid #!/bin/bash #PBS -W group_list=bh_class #PBS -q windfall #PBS -l jobtype=cluster_only #PBS -l select=1:ncpus=2:mem=4gb #PBS -l walltime=24:00:00 #PBS -l cput=24:00:00 #PBS -l place=pack:shared #PBS -M netid@email.arizona.edu #PBS -m bea Replace netid

steps.title: Submit run-interactive.sh interactively using qsub
steps.instruction: Submit run-interactive.sh interactively using qsub: qsub -I run-interactive.sh The capital

steps.title: Once the job is ready move...
steps.instruction: Once the job is ready move back into your class directory. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username Use YOUR username

steps.title: Move into your assembly directory which...
steps.instruction: Move into your assembly directory which contains your contigs: Note: Its possible your contigs are in another directory (megahit-out). Move to the directory where final_contigs.fa are located. Remember final_contigs.fa is the combined assemblies from your partner. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/assembly

steps.title: Load prodigal and run it on your fixed_contigs
steps.instruction: Load prodigal and run it on your fixed_contigs.fa to generate gene calls. Since final_contigs.fa may be located somewhere other than the assembly directory, you can write out the full paths to make sure the output goes where its suppose to: $ prodigal -i /path/to/final_contigs.fa -o /rsgrps/bh_class/username/prodigal/gene_calls -a /rsgrps/bh_class/username/prodigal/proteins.faa -d /rsgrps/bh_class/username/prodigal/nucleotides.fna $ module load prodigal/2.6.2$ prodigal -i fixed_contigs.fa -o ../prodigal/gene_calls -a ../prodigal/proteins.faa -d ../prodigal/nucleotides.fna IMPORTANT: make sure to run prodigal on the fixed_contigs.fa file which was generated when simplifying the fasta header lines.The output will be placed in the prodigal directory that was created and the file name will be gene_calls in addition to a file containing the protein (proteins.faa) and nucleotide (nucleotide.faa) sequences for the genes. $ module load prodigal/2.6.2 $ prodigal -i fixed_contigs.fa -o ../prodigal/gene_calls -a ../prodigal/proteins.faa -d ../prodigal/nucleotides.fna IMPORTANT: make sure to run prodigal on the fixed_contigs.fa file which was generated when simplifying the fasta header lines. The output will be placed in the prodigal directory that was created and the file name will be gene_calls in addition to a file containing the protein (proteins.faa) and nucleotide (nucleotide.faa) sequences for the genes.

steps.title: Move into your prodigal directory and...
steps.instruction: Move into your prodigal directory and make sure the gene calls were generated. $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/prodigal$ head gene_calls $ cd /rsgrps/bh_class/username/prodigal $ head gene_calls

steps.title: Use your scripting or unix skills...
steps.instruction: Use your scripting or unix skills to detect how many genes were detected on each of the assemblies. Create a table in google documents that shows the number of "ORFs" or open reading frames detected on each assembly. We will add addiitonal data to this table later on # of genes with annotation, so the table should have a single column for #ORFs and the rows should be the name of each sample "SRR..."
